draft.introduction: Mark’s “Little Apocalypse”: Jesus predicts the Temple’s destruction and the coming of the Son of Man. The discourse blends warnings about the fall of Jerusalem with cosmic eschatological imagery.

translation.text: And as he came out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Look, Teacher, what wonderful stones and what wonderful buildings!” And Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone upon another that will not be thrown down.” Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. These are but the beginning of the birth pains.
translation.original_text: οὐ μὴ ἀφεθῇ ὡδε λίθος ἐπὶ λίθῳ

translation.text: But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light, and the stars will be falling from heaven. And then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory. And then he will send out the angels and gather his elect from the four winds.
translation.original_text: καὶ τότε ὄψονται τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐρχόμενον ἐν νεφέλαις

translator_notes.note: “The Son of Man coming in clouds” directly quotes Daniel 7:13 LXX. The title “Son of Man” (υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου) is Jesus’ preferred self-designation in Mark, appearing fourteen times. It combines human vulnerability with divine authority.
